title: Choose a persistence mode
description: Pick RDB, AOF, or both based on the acceptable data-loss window and restart-time tradeoffs.
body: Steps: 1. Define the acceptable data-loss window on a crash. 2. RDB takes periodic point-in-time snapshots — compact and fast to restore but loses everything since the last snapshot. 3. AOF logs every write and replays on restart — far smaller loss window (especially appendfsync everysec or always) but a larger file and slower load. 4. For durability, enable AOF (often with RDB for fast restore); for a pure cache, persistence may be off entirely. 5. Verify restart actually reloads the expected data. Rollback point: revert config and restart. Validation: a kill-and-restart test loses no more than the chosen window and the instance reloads the dataset; risk boundary — RDB-only on a datastore role can lose minutes of writes.
